title: A Storm Simile.
content:
("Oh, regardez le ciel!")

    [June, 1828.]


    See, where on high the moving masses, piled
    By the wind, break in groups grotesque and wild,
        Present strange shapes to view;
    Oft flares a pallid flash from out their shrouds,
    As though some air-born giant 'mid the clouds
        Sudden his falchion drew.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Victor-Marie Hugo, titled "A Storm Simile.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
